Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The goal Exclude any case that contains a specific sequence of three activities in direct succession Approve Purchase Requisition then Create Order then Confirm Order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore query should return all events from event_log except those belonging to cases that have that sequence with the timing condition

Name: Validation - PE
Text: direct succession means no other event from same case in between them

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possibly use window functions Lag function

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: if rows are ordered by timestamp then the three events should be consecutive in the ordering

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: using self join or partition windows and compare row numbers

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then final SELECT FROM event_log where case_id not in list of case_ids that satisfy these conditions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: if one sequence appears multiple times its still the same case id

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I can write a subquery that identifies all distinct case ids that have at least one occurrence of these sequences with time difference more than 5 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WITH numbered AS SELECT ROW_NUMBER OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp as rn FROM event_log

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: find sequences join numbered rows where rn plus 1 and rn plus 2 and activities are Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: more than 5 days means strictly greater than 5 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WHERE n3 timestamp minus n2 timestamp greater than INTERVAL 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The query should return all events from event_log that do not belong to these case ids

Name: Validation - PE
Text: using row_number ensures that the three events are consecutive rows because we use condition rn plus 1 for each join
